steps.title: Identify the sample and its population
steps.content:
• The psychologist chose a random sample of 35 students.
  • The sample was taken from all students at one middle school in a large city.
  • This defines the population from which the sample was drawn.

steps.title: Determine the largest group for generalization
steps.content:
• Because the sample is random, the results can be generalized to the population from which it was drawn, i.e., all students at that school.
  • Since only one school was sampled, generalization to all middle schools in the city or all students in the city is not justified.
  • Therefore the largest group to which the results can be generalized is all students at the school.
  • That confirms B is correct.
